sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Good, the game restarted. The state is now "NOT_FINISHED". Let me check what changed - the shapes should be back in their original positions and the bars reset.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after restart
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Yellow shapes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, center=({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f})")

print("\nRight panel bars:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    if 32 <= min_r <= 50 and 34 <= min_c <= 63 and n['pixels'] <= 5:
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        print(f"  ({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f}): {n['color']}")

print("\nLeft panel bars:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    if 32 <= min_r <= 50 and 1 <= min_c <= 30 and n['pixels'] <= 5:
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        print(f"  ({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f}): {n['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, the game restarted. The bars are all reset to white (inactive), and the yellow shapes are back at their original positions (8, 46) and (25, 46).
Now let me think about what the correct move should be. The left panel has row 1 all active (B B B B). This might be the "target" pattern that I need to reproduce on the right panel.
Let me try setting the right panel row 1 to all active (B B B B) and then clicking the blue circle.
But wait, I tried setting all bars to active before and it caused game over. The difference is that before, I set ALL 24 bars to active. Now I'm only setting row 1 (4 bars) to active, matching the left panel pattern.
Let me try this: set right panel row 1 (33, 39), (33, 44), (33, 49), (33, 54) to B, then click blue circle.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Game restarted. Left panel has row 1 all active (B B B B). Right panel bars all reset to white. Yellow shapes at (8, 46) and (25, 46).
Hypothesis: Left panel bars represent target pattern; right panel bars need to match. Set right panel row 1 to match left panel row 1.
Plan: Set right panel row 1 cols 1-4 to B, then click blue circle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Set right panel row 1 to match left panel row 1 (all B)
# Then click blue circle
actions = [
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 39},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 44},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 49},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 54},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 46}
]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
